1: //! Build and parse PR bodies with HTML-comment markers for ez-managed sections.
2: //!
3: //! ## Marker structure
4: //!
5: //! ```text
6: //! <user body — never touched>
7: //!
8: //! <!-- ez:begin — remove this line to disable ez edits -->
9: //! <!-- below this line automated updates may occur -->
10: //!
11: //! <!-- ez:summary:begin -->
12: //! ...
13: //! <!-- ez:summary:end -->
14: //!
15: //! <!-- ez:references:begin -->
16: //! ...
17: //! <!-- ez:references:end -->
18: //!
19: //! <!-- ez:stack:begin -->
20: //! ...
21: //! <!-- ez:stack:end -->
22: //!
23: //! <!-- above this line automated updates may occur -->
24: //! <!-- ez:end — remove this line to disable ez edits -->
25: //! ```
26: 
27: use crate::stack::StackState;
28: 
29: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
30: // Marker constants
31: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
32: 
33: pub const EZ_BEGIN: &str = "<!-- ez:begin \u{2014} remove this line to disable ez edits -->";
34: pub const EZ_BELOW: &str = "<!-- below this line automated updates may occur -->";
35: pub const EZ_ABOVE: &str = "<!-- above this line automated updates may occur -->";
36: pub const EZ_END: &str = "<!-- ez:end \u{2014} remove this line to disable ez edits -->";
37: 
38: pub const SUMMARY_BEGIN: &str = "<!-- ez:summary:begin -->";
39: pub const SUMMARY_END: &str = "<!-- ez:summary:end -->";
40: pub const REFERENCES_BEGIN: &str = "<!-- ez:references:begin -->";
41: pub const REFERENCES_END: &str = "<!-- ez:references:end -->";
42: pub const STACK_BEGIN: &str = "<!-- ez:stack:begin -->";
43: pub const STACK_END: &str = "<!-- ez:stack:end -->";
44: 
45: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46: // Legacy compat — keep old AncestorPr + build_stack_body for any callers
47: // that haven't migrated yet.
48: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
49: 
50: /// One upstream ancestor branch and its PR info (if any).
51: pub struct AncestorPr {
52:     pub branch: String,
53:     pub pr_number: Option<u64>,
54:     pub pr_url: Option<String>,
55: }
56: 
57: /// Returns the markdown stack section, or None if no ancestors have a PR number.
58: pub fn build_stack_section(ancestors: &[AncestorPr]) -> Option<String> {
59:     let linked: Vec<String> = ancestors
60:         .iter()
61:         .filter_map(|a| a.pr_number.map(|num| (a, num)))
62:         .enumerate()
63:         .map(|(i, (a, num))| match &a.pr_url {
64:             Some(url) => format!("{}. [{} #{}]({})", i + 1, a.branch, num, url),
65:             None => format!("{}. {} #{}", i + 1, a.branch, num),
66:         })
67:         .collect();
68: 
69:     if linked.is_empty() {
70:         None
71:     } else {
72:         Some(format!("**Stack:**\n{}", linked.join("\n")))
73:     }
74: }
75: 
76: /// Legacy: build a PR body with just the stack section appended.
77: pub fn build_stack_body(ancestors: &[AncestorPr], user_body: &str) -> String {
78:     match build_stack_section(ancestors) {
79:         Some(section) => format!("{}\n\n---\n\n{}", user_body, section),
80:         None => user_body.to_string(),
81:     }
82: }
83: 
84: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
85: // Stack node for full tree rendering
86: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
87: 
88: /// A node in the stack tree for rendering.
89: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
90: pub struct StackNode {
91:     pub branch: String,
92:     pub pr_number: Option<u64>,
93:     pub pr_url: Option<String>,
94:     pub is_current: bool,
95:     pub children: Vec<StackNode>,
96: }
97: 
98: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
99: // Full tree building
100: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101: 
102: /// Build the full stack tree for the chain containing `current_branch`.
103: ///
104: /// Only includes branches in the same stack chain (ancestors up to trunk
105: /// and all descendants), not unrelated branches off trunk.
106: pub fn build_full_tree(state: &StackState, current_branch: &str) -> Vec<StackNode> {
107:     // Walk up from current_branch to find the bottom branch (first child of trunk).
108:     let path = state.path_to_trunk(current_branch);
109:     // path is [current, ..., trunk]. The second-to-last is the bottom branch.
110:     let bottom = if path.len() >= 2 {
111:         path[path.len() - 2].clone()
112:     } else {
113:         current_branch.to_string()
114:     };
115:     // Build tree from the bottom branch only.
116:     vec![build_subtree(state, &bottom, current_branch)]
117: }
118: 
119: fn build_subtree(state: &StackState, branch: &str, current_branch: &str) -> StackNode {
120:     let meta = state.branches.get(branch);
121:     let pr_number = meta.and_then(|m| m.pr_number);
122:     let pr_url = pr_url_for_branch(state, branch);
123:     let children = state.children_of(branch);
124:     StackNode {
125:         branch: branch.to_string(),
126:         pr_number,
127:         pr_url,
128:         is_current: branch == current_branch,
129:         children: children
130:             .iter()
131:             .map(|c| build_subtree(state, c, current_branch))
132:             .collect(),
133:     }
134: }
135: 
136: /// Compute the full PR URL for a branch using metadata.
137: fn pr_url_for_branch(state: &StackState, branch: &str) -> Option<String> {
138:     pr_url_for_branch_pub(state, branch)
139: }
140: 
141: /// Public version of `pr_url_for_branch` for use by other modules.
142: pub fn pr_url_for_branch_pub(state: &StackState, branch: &str) -> Option<String> {
143:     let meta = state.branches.get(branch)?;
144:     let pr_number = meta.pr_number?;
145:     // Use the branch's effective PR repo to construct the URL.
146:     let repo = meta
147:         .pr_repo
148:         .clone()
149:         .or_else(|| state.effective_pr_repo(branch))
150:         .or_else(|| state.repo.clone());
151:     repo.map(|r| format!("https://github.com/{}/pull/{}", r, pr_number))
152: }
153: 
154: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
155: // Full tree rendering
156: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
157: 
158: /// Count total branches (with PRs) in a tree for the header.
159: fn count_nodes(nodes: &[StackNode]) -> usize {
160:     nodes
161:         .iter()
162:         .map(|n| 1 + count_nodes(&n.children))
163:         .sum()
164: }
165: 
166: /// Find the 1-based position of the current branch in the tree (depth-first).
167: fn find_position(nodes: &[StackNode], counter: &mut usize) -> Option<usize> {
168:     for node in nodes {
169:         *counter += 1;
170:         if node.is_current {
171:             return Some(*counter);
172:         }
173:         if let Some(pos) = find_position(&node.children, counter) {
174:             return Some(pos);
175:         }
176:     }
177:     None
178: }
179: 
180: /// Render the full stack tree as markdown.
181: ///
182: /// Linear sections use numbered lists. Branch points use indented sub-lists.
183: pub fn render_full_tree(roots: &[StackNode]) -> String {
184:     let total = count_nodes(roots);
185:     let mut counter = 0;
186:     let position = find_position(roots, &mut counter).unwrap_or(0);
187: 
188:     let mut lines = Vec::new();
189:     lines.push(format!(
190:         "**Stack** (this PR is {} of {}):",
191:         position, total
192:     ));
193: 
194:     let mut num = 1;
195:     render_nodes(&mut lines, roots, &mut num, 0);
196: 
197:     lines.join("\n")
198: }
199: 
200: fn render_nodes(lines: &mut Vec<String>, nodes: &[StackNode], num: &mut usize, indent: usize) {
201:     let prefix = "  ".repeat(indent);
202:     let is_sublist = indent > 0 && nodes.len() > 1;
203: 
204:     for node in nodes {
205:         let pr_link = match (&node.pr_number, &node.pr_url) {
206:             (Some(n), Some(url)) => {
207:                 // Extract short repo name for display: "owner/repo" → "owner#N"
208:                 let short = url
209:                     .trim_start_matches("https://github.com/")
210:                     .split('/')
211:                     .next()
212:                     .unwrap_or("?");
213:                 format!(" ([{}#{}]({}))", short, n, url)
214:             }
215:             (Some(n), None) => format!(" (#{n})"),
216:             _ => String::new(),
217:         };
218: 
219:         let branch_text = if node.is_current {
220:             format!("**{}** \u{2190} you are here{}", node.branch, pr_link)
221:         } else {
222:             format!("{}{}", node.branch, pr_link)
223:         };
224: 
225:         if is_sublist {
226:             // Sub-list item (branch point children)
227:             lines.push(format!("{}- {}", prefix, branch_text));
228:         } else {
229:             // Numbered list item
230:             lines.push(format!("{}{}. {}", prefix, num, branch_text));
231:             *num += 1;
232:         }
233: 
234:         // Recurse into children.
235:         if !node.children.is_empty() {
236:             if node.children.len() == 1 {
237:                 // Linear continuation — keep numbering.
238:                 render_nodes(lines, &node.children, num, indent);
239:             } else {
240:                 // Branch point — sub-list.
241:                 render_nodes(lines, &node.children, num, indent + 1);
242:             }
243:         }
244:     }
245: }
246: 
247: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
248: // Marker-based body parsing and building
249: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
250: 
251: /// Parsed sections of an ez-managed PR body.
252: #[derive(Debug, Default)]
253: pub struct PrBodySections {
254:     /// Content above `<!-- ez:begin -->`.
255:     pub user_body: String,
256:     /// Whether the outer ez markers are present (both begin AND end).
257:     pub has_ez_markers: bool,
258:     /// Content between summary markers, if present.
259:     pub summary: Option<String>,
260:     /// Content between references markers, if present.
261:     pub references: Option<String>,
262:     /// Content between stack markers, if present.
263:     pub stack: Option<String>,
264:     /// Whether the summary subsection markers exist.
265:     pub has_summary_markers: bool,
266:     /// Whether the references subsection markers exist.
267:     pub has_references_markers: bool,
268:     /// Whether the stack subsection markers exist.
269:     pub has_stack_markers: bool,
270: }
271: 
272: /// Check if the ez outer markers are present in a PR body.
273: pub fn has_ez_markers(body: &str) -> bool {
274:     // We check for the distinctive prefix only, not the full line with em-dash,
275:     // in case of minor formatting differences.
276:     body.contains("<!-- ez:begin") && body.contains("<!-- ez:end")
277: }
278: 
279: /// Parse a PR body into its sections.
280: pub fn parse_ez_body(body: &str) -> PrBodySections {
281:     let mut sections = PrBodySections::default();
282: 
283:     // Find the ez:begin marker line.
284:     let begin_prefix = "<!-- ez:begin";
285:     let end_prefix = "<!-- ez:end";
286: 
287:     let begin_pos = body.find(begin_prefix);
288:     let end_pos = body.find(end_prefix);
289: 
290:     match (begin_pos, end_pos) {
291:         (Some(bp), Some(_ep)) => {
292:             sections.has_ez_markers = true;
293:             // User body is everything before the begin marker.
294:             sections.user_body = body[..bp].trim_end().to_string();
295: 
296:             // Parse subsections within the ez block.
297:             sections.summary = extract_subsection(body, SUMMARY_BEGIN, SUMMARY_END);
298:             sections.has_summary_markers =
299:                 body.contains(SUMMARY_BEGIN) && body.contains(SUMMARY_END);
300:             sections.references = extract_subsection(body, REFERENCES_BEGIN, REFERENCES_END);
301:             sections.has_references_markers =
302:                 body.contains(REFERENCES_BEGIN) && body.contains(REFERENCES_END);
303:             sections.stack = extract_subsection(body, STACK_BEGIN, STACK_END);
304:             sections.has_stack_markers =
305:                 body.contains(STACK_BEGIN) && body.contains(STACK_END);
306:         }
307:         _ => {
308:             // No ez markers — entire body is user content.
309:             sections.user_body = body.to_string();
310:         }
311:     }
312: 
313:     sections
314: }
315: 
316: /// Extract content between two marker lines.
317: fn extract_subsection(body: &str, begin_marker: &str, end_marker: &str) -> Option<String> {
318:     let begin_pos = body.find(begin_marker)?;
319:     let after_begin = begin_pos + begin_marker.len();
320:     let end_pos = body[after_begin..].find(end_marker)?;
321:     let content = body[after_begin..after_begin + end_pos].trim();
322:     if content.is_empty() {
323:         None
324:     } else {
325:         Some(content.to_string())
326:     }
327: }
328: 
329: /// Build a complete ez-managed PR body from sections.
330: ///
331: /// `user_body`: user-written content (above the markers).
332: /// `summary`: content for the summary section (if None, uses default).
333: /// `references`: content for the references section (if None, section omitted).
334: /// `stack_tree`: content for the stack section.
335: pub fn build_ez_body(
336:     user_body: &str,
337:     summary: Option<&str>,
338:     references: Option<&str>,
339:     stack_tree: &str,
340: ) -> String {
341:     let mut parts = Vec::new();
342: 
343:     // User body.
344:     let trimmed_user = user_body.trim();
345:     if !trimmed_user.is_empty() {
346:         parts.push(trimmed_user.to_string());
347:         parts.push(String::new());
348:     }
349: 
350:     // Outer begin marker.
351:     parts.push(EZ_BEGIN.to_string());
352:     parts.push(EZ_BELOW.to_string());
353:     parts.push(String::new());
354: 
355:     // Summary section.
356:     if let Some(s) = summary {
357:         parts.push(SUMMARY_BEGIN.to_string());
358:         parts.push(s.to_string());
359:         parts.push(SUMMARY_END.to_string());
360:         parts.push(String::new());
361:     }
362: 
363:     // References section.
364:     if let Some(r) = references {
365:         parts.push(REFERENCES_BEGIN.to_string());
366:         parts.push(r.to_string());
367:         parts.push(REFERENCES_END.to_string());
368:         parts.push(String::new());
369:     }
370: 
371:     // Stack section.
372:     parts.push(STACK_BEGIN.to_string());
373:     parts.push(stack_tree.to_string());
374:     parts.push(STACK_END.to_string());
375: 
376:     parts.push(String::new());
377:     // Outer end marker.
378:     parts.push(EZ_ABOVE.to_string());
379:     parts.push(EZ_END.to_string());
380: 
381:     parts.join("\n")
382: }
383: 
384: /// Update only the stack subsection in an existing PR body.
385: ///
386: /// Preserves user body, summary, and references. Only replaces the content
387: /// between `<!-- ez:stack:begin -->` and `<!-- ez:stack:end -->`.
388: ///
389: /// Returns `None` if the body doesn't have ez markers or stack markers.
390: pub fn update_stack_section_only(body: &str, new_stack: &str) -> Option<String> {
391:     if !has_ez_markers(body) {
392:         return None;
393:     }
394: 
395:     let stack_begin_pos = body.find(STACK_BEGIN)?;
396:     let after_begin = stack_begin_pos + STACK_BEGIN.len();
397:     let stack_end_pos_relative = body[after_begin..].find(STACK_END)?;
398:     let stack_end_pos = after_begin + stack_end_pos_relative;
399: 
400:     let mut result = String::new();
401:     result.push_str(&body[..after_begin]);
402:     result.push('\n');
403:     result.push_str(new_stack);
404:     result.push('\n');
405:     result.push_str(&body[stack_end_pos..]);
406: 
407:     Some(result)
408: }
409: 
410: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
411: // Contiguous PR chain detection
412: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
413: 
414: /// Find all branches in the contiguous PR chain touching `branch`.
415: ///
416: /// Walks up through ancestors and down through children, stopping at any
417: /// branch that doesn't have a PR number. Returns branch names.
418: pub fn contiguous_pr_chain(state: &StackState, branch: &str) -> Vec<String> {
419:     let mut chain = Vec::new();
420:     let mut visited = std::collections::HashSet::new();
421: 
422:     // Walk up to trunk (stopping at gaps).
423:     let path = state.path_to_trunk(branch);
424:     for b in &path {
425:         if state.is_trunk(b) {
426:             break;
427:         }
428:         let has_pr = state
429:             .branches
430:             .get(b.as_str())
431:             .and_then(|m| m.pr_number)
432:             .is_some();
433:         if has_pr {
434:             chain.push(b.clone());
435:             visited.insert(b.clone());
436:         } else if b != branch {
437:             // Gap in ancestors — stop going up.
438:             break;
439:         }
440:     }
441: 
442:     // Walk down through children (stopping at gaps).
443:     fn walk_children(
444:         state: &StackState,
445:         branch: &str,
446:         chain: &mut Vec<String>,
447:         visited: &mut std::collections::HashSet<String>,
448:     ) {
449:         for child in state.children_of(branch) {
450:             if visited.contains(&child) {
451:                 continue;
452:             }
453:             let has_pr = state
454:                 .branches
455:                 .get(child.as_str())
456:                 .and_then(|m| m.pr_number)
457:                 .is_some();
458:             if has_pr {
459:                 visited.insert(child.clone());
460:                 chain.push(child.clone());
461:                 walk_children(state, &child, chain, visited);
462:             }
463:             // Gap — don't continue past this child.
464:         }
465:     }
466: 
467:     walk_children(state, branch, &mut chain, &mut visited);
468: 
469:     chain
470: }
471:

476: #[cfg(test)]
477: mod tests {
478:     use super::*;
479:     use crate::stack::StackState;
480: 
481:     fn anc(branch: &str, number: Option<u64>, url: Option<&str>) -> AncestorPr {
482:         AncestorPr {
483:             branch: branch.to_string(),
484:             pr_number: number,
485:             pr_url: url.map(|s| s.to_string()),
486:         }
487:     }

489:     // --- Legacy build_stack_section tests ---
490: 
491:     #[test]
492:     fn section_empty_ancestors_returns_none() {
493:         assert!(build_stack_section(&[]).is_none());
494:     }
495: 
496:     #[test]
497:     fn section_ancestor_without_pr_returns_none() {
498:         let ancestors = vec![anc("feat/a", None, None)];
499:         assert!(build_stack_section(&ancestors).is_none());
500:     }
501: 
502:     #[test]
503:     fn section_one_ancestor_with_url() {
504:         let ancestors = vec![anc(
505:             "feat/a",
506:             Some(101),
507:             Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/101"),
508:         )];
509:         let section = build_stack_section(&ancestors).unwrap();
510:         assert_eq!(
511:             section,
512:             "**Stack:**\n1. [feat/a #101](https://github.com/org/repo/pull/101)"
513:         );
514:     }
515: 
516:     #[test]
517:     fn section_one_ancestor_without_url() {
518:         let ancestors = vec![anc("feat/a", Some(101), None)];
519:         let section = build_stack_section(&ancestors).unwrap();
520:         assert_eq!(section, "**Stack:**\n1. feat/a #101");
521:     }
522: 
523:     #[test]
524:     fn section_skips_ancestors_without_pr_number() {
525:         let ancestors = vec![
526:             anc(
527:                 "feat/a",

529:                 Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/101"),
530:             ),
531:             anc("feat/b", None, None),
532:             anc(
533:                 "feat/c",
534:                 Some(103),
535:                 Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/103"),
536:             ),
537:         ];
538:         let section = build_stack_section(&ancestors).unwrap();
539:         assert_eq!(
540:             section,
541:             "**Stack:**\n1. [feat/a #101](https://github.com/org/repo/pull/101)\n2. [feat/c #103](https://github.com/org/repo/pull/103)"
542:         );
543:     }
544: 
545:     #[test]
546:     fn section_numbers_are_sequential_for_linked_only() {
547:         let ancestors = vec![
548:             anc("feat/a", None, None),
549:             anc(
550:                 "feat/b",
551:                 Some(102),
552:                 Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/102"),
553:             ),
554:             anc(
555:                 "feat/c",
556:                 Some(103),
557:                 Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/103"),
558:             ),
559:         ];
560:         let section = build_stack_section(&ancestors).unwrap();
561:         assert!(section.contains("1. [feat/b"));
562:         assert!(section.contains("2. [feat/c"));

565:     #[test]
566:     fn body_no_ancestors_returns_user_body_unchanged() {
567:         let result = build_stack_body(&[], "My PR description.");
568:         assert_eq!(result, "My PR description.");
569:     }

595:         let body = "This PR adds X.\n\nMore details here.";

601:     // --- Marker tests ---
602: 
603:     #[test]
604:     fn has_ez_markers_detects_both() {
605:         let body = format!("hello\n\n{}\nstuff\n{}", EZ_BEGIN, EZ_END);
606:         assert!(has_ez_markers(&body));

611:         assert!(!has_ez_markers("just a plain body"));
612:     }

615:     fn has_ez_markers_false_when_only_begin() {

617:         assert!(!has_ez_markers(&body));

620:     #[test]
621:     fn parse_ez_body_no_markers() {
622:         let parsed = parse_ez_body("plain body text");
623:         assert!(!parsed.has_ez_markers);
624:         assert_eq!(parsed.user_body, "plain body text");
625:         assert!(parsed.summary.is_none());

628: 
629:     #[test]
630:     fn parse_ez_body_with_all_sections() {
631:         let body = format!(
632:             "My custom description\n\n{}\n{}\n\n{}\nPart of a stack.\n{}\n\n{}\nStack content\n{}\n\n{}\n{}",
633:             EZ_BEGIN, EZ_BELOW, SUMMARY_BEGIN, SUMMARY_END, STACK_BEGIN, STACK_END, EZ_ABOVE, EZ_END
634:         );
635:         let parsed = parse_ez_body(&body);
636:         assert!(parsed.has_ez_markers);
637:         assert_eq!(parsed.user_body, "My custom description");
638:         assert_eq!(parsed.summary.as_deref(), Some("Part of a stack."));
639:         assert!(parsed.has_summary_markers);
640:         assert_eq!(parsed.stack.as_deref(), Some("Stack content"));
641:         assert!(parsed.has_stack_markers);
642:     }
643: 
644:     #[test]
645:     fn parse_ez_body_missing_summary_markers() {
646:         let body = format!(
647:             "desc\n\n{}\n{}\n\n{}\nStack here\n{}\n\n{}\n{}",
648:             EZ_BEGIN, EZ_BELOW, STACK_BEGIN, STACK_END, EZ_ABOVE, EZ_END
649:         );
650:         let parsed = parse_ez_body(&body);
651:         assert!(parsed.has_ez_markers);
652:         assert!(!parsed.has_summary_markers);
653:         assert!(parsed.summary.is_none());
654:         assert!(parsed.has_stack_markers);
655:     }

659:     #[test]
660:     fn build_ez_body_all_sections() {
661:         let body = build_ez_body(
662:             "My description",
663:             Some("Summary text"),
664:             Some("Ref content"),
665:             "Stack tree",
666:         );
667:         assert!(body.starts_with("My description"));
668:         assert!(body.contains(EZ_BEGIN));
669:         assert!(body.contains(SUMMARY_BEGIN));
670:         assert!(body.contains("Summary text"));
671:         assert!(body.contains(SUMMARY_END));
672:         assert!(body.contains(REFERENCES_BEGIN));
673:         assert!(body.contains("Ref content"));
674:         assert!(body.contains(REFERENCES_END));
675:         assert!(body.contains(STACK_BEGIN));
676:         assert!(body.contains("Stack tree"));
677:         assert!(body.contains(STACK_END));
678:         assert!(body.contains(EZ_END));
679:         assert!(body.contains(EZ_BELOW));
680:         assert!(body.contains(EZ_ABOVE));
681:     }

703:         let updated = update_stack_section_only(&original, "new stack").unwrap();
704:         assert!(updated.contains("new stack"));

717:     #[test]
718:     fn render_linear_tree() {
719:         let nodes = vec![StackNode {
720:             branch: "feat/a".to_string(),
721:             pr_number: Some(1),
722:             pr_url: Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/1".to_string()),
723:             is_current: false,
724:             children: vec![StackNode {
725:                 branch: "feat/b".to_string(),
726:                 pr_number: Some(2),
727:                 pr_url: Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/2".to_string()),
728:                 is_current: true,
729:                 children: vec![],
730:             }],
731:         }];
732:         let rendered = render_full_tree(&nodes);
733:         assert!(rendered.contains("this PR is 2 of 2"));
734:         assert!(rendered.contains("1. feat/a"));
735:         assert!(rendered.contains("2. **feat/b** \u{2190} you are here"));
736:     }
737: 
738:     #[test]
739:     fn render_branching_tree() {
740:         let nodes = vec![StackNode {
741:             branch: "feat/a".to_string(),
742:             pr_number: Some(1),
743:             pr_url: Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/1".to_string()),
744:             is_current: true,
745:             children: vec![
746:                 StackNode {
747:                     branch: "feat/b".to_string(),
748:                     pr_number: Some(2),
749:                     pr_url: Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/2".to_string()),
750:                     is_current: false,
751:                     children: vec![],
752:                 },
753:                 StackNode {
754:                     branch: "feat/c".to_string(),
755:                     pr_number: Some(3),
756:                     pr_url: Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/3".to_string()),
757:                     is_current: false,
758:                     children: vec![],
759:                 },
760:             ],
761:         }];
762:         let rendered = render_full_tree(&nodes);
763:         assert!(rendered.contains("this PR is 1 of 3"));
764:         assert!(rendered.contains("**feat/a** \u{2190} you are here"));
765:         // Children should be sub-list items.
766:         assert!(rendered.contains("- feat/b"));
767:         assert!(rendered.contains("- feat/c"));
768:     }

798:     // --- Contiguous PR chain tests ---
799: 
800:     #[test]
801:     fn contiguous_chain_linear_all_prs() {
802:         let mut state = StackState::new("main".to_string());
803:         state.add_branch("a", "main", "aaa", None, None);
804:         state.add_branch("b", "a", "bbb", None, None);
805:         state.add_branch("c", "b", "ccc", None, None);
806:         state.get_branch_mut("a").unwrap().pr_number = Some(1);
807:         state.get_branch_mut("b").unwrap().pr_number = Some(2);
808:         state.get_branch_mut("c").unwrap().pr_number = Some(3);
809: 
810:         let chain = contiguous_pr_chain(&state, "b");
811:         assert!(chain.contains(&"a".to_string()));
812:         assert!(chain.contains(&"b".to_string()));
813:         assert!(chain.contains(&"c".to_string()));
814:     }
815: 
816:     #[test]
817:     fn contiguous_chain_gap_stops_ancestor() {
818:         let mut state = StackState::new("main".to_string());
819:         state.add_branch("a", "main", "aaa", None, None);
820:         state.add_branch("b", "a", "bbb", None, None);
821:         state.add_branch("c", "b", "ccc", None, None);
822:         // a has no PR — gap
823:         state.get_branch_mut("b").unwrap().pr_number = Some(2);
824:         state.get_branch_mut("c").unwrap().pr_number = Some(3);
825: 
826:         let chain = contiguous_pr_chain(&state, "c");
827:         // Should not include a (no PR) but should include b and c.
828:         assert!(!chain.contains(&"a".to_string()));
829:         assert!(chain.contains(&"b".to_string()));
830:         assert!(chain.contains(&"c".to_string()));
831:     }
832: 
833:     #[test]
834:     fn contiguous_chain_gap_stops_children() {
835:         let mut state = StackState::new("main".to_string());
836:         state.add_branch("a", "main", "aaa", None, None);
837:         state.add_branch("b", "a", "bbb", None, None);
838:         state.add_branch("c", "b", "ccc", None, None);
839:         state.get_branch_mut("a").unwrap().pr_number = Some(1);
840:         // b has no PR — gap
841:         state.get_branch_mut("c").unwrap().pr_number = Some(3);
842: 
843:         let chain = contiguous_pr_chain(&state, "a");
844:         assert!(chain.contains(&"a".to_string()));
845:         // b has no PR, so c (past the gap) should NOT be included.
846:         assert!(!chain.contains(&"b".to_string()));
847:         assert!(!chain.contains(&"c".to_string()));
848:     }
849: }
